danger_signals.meaning: hazard requiring course adjustment; penalty zone

danger_signals.visual_form: water bodies positioned on course layout
danger_signals.meaning: major obstacle; loss condition if ball enters
physical barrier; ball loss or trajectory block

safety_signals.visual_form: clear, open grass corridor between tee and green
safety_signals.meaning: optimal play zone; straight shot opportunity

safety_signals.visual_form: minimal obstacle arrangement in course layout

faction_indicators.environmental_markers: bunkers and super rough terrain; PGA West Stadium Course aesthetic
water bodies and tight boundaries; distinct course layout philosophy

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: obstacle density increases from straightforward fairways to elaborate sand trap arrangements
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: player must adapt shot strategy and club selection based on visible terrain complexity

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: United States course emphasizes bunker clusters; Japan course emphasizes water hazards and spatial constraints
photorealistic country club lodge with setting sun and surrounding trees as end-state imagery
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: visual reward for successful course completion; environmental storytelling of leisure achievement

environmental_storytelling.example: United States course visually dominated by sand bunkers (PGA West Stadium Course aesthetic) vs Japan course emphasizing water obstacles and tight fairway boundaries
photorealistic country club lodge against setting sun surrounded by trees communicates achievement destination and social prestige of golf completion
progression from relatively straightforward fairways to elaborate arrangements of sand traps signals escalating challenge through visual obstacle accumulation

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: limited shot range but easiest aiming guide control; beginner-friendly character archetype
average range and control attributes; balanced playstyle
average range and control attributes; balanced playstyle with celebrity endorsement identity
best shot range but worst aiming control; risk-reward character requiring precision timing

notes: Game lacks traditional faction conflict despite title reference to 'fighting.' World-state is communicated primarily through course design, environmental hazard placement, and end-state visual rewards rather than dynamic NPC behavior or political signaling. Character selection creates implicit difficulty/playstyle tiers rather than faction identities. Regional courses (USA vs Japan) establish geographic competition framework without narrative conflict.
